From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American mixed martial arts fighter (born 1986)

**Dennis Ross Bermudez**[[3]](#cite_note-3) (born December 13, 1986) is a retired American [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") who competed in the [featherweight](/wiki/Featherweight_(MMA) "Featherweight (MMA)") division of the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC). A professional competitor from 2009 to 2019, he made a name for himself mainly fighting on the [East Coast](/wiki/East_Coast_of_the_United_States "East Coast of the United States"), and was a competitor on [Spike TV](/wiki/Spike_TV "Spike TV")'s [The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Bisping_vs._Team_Miller "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller"), where he became the runner-up.

## Wrestling

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Bermudez&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Wrestling")]

Bermudez began wrestling in 2000. Coached by Scott Wickham,[[4]](#cite_note-4) he held a [high school](/wiki/Scholastic_wrestling "Scholastic wrestling") record of 112-23.[[5]](#cite_note-TUF14fighters-5) From high school, he went on to [wrestle](/wiki/Collegiate_wrestling "Collegiate wrestling") at [Bloomsburg University of Pennsylvania](/wiki/Bloomsburg_University_of_Pennsylvania "Bloomsburg University of Pennsylvania") where in 2007 he was ranked 22nd in the nation at the Division 1 level. Bermudez then became a college free-style All-American during a match in [Akron](/wiki/Akron "Akron"), [Ohio](/wiki/Ohio "Ohio").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Bermudez&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Bermudez&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Early career")]

Bermudez first started training [mixed martial arts](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts") in [Saugerties, New York](/wiki/Saugerties,_New_York "Saugerties, New York"), his home town, in 2009. He later went on to train at Blackman MMA in [Harrisburg](/wiki/Harrisburg "Harrisburg"), [Pennsylvania](/wiki/Pennsylvania "Pennsylvania"). He trained at Blackman MMA for nearly two years as he competed as an amateur and professional. Bermudez competed in four amateur bouts before earning his professional license, finishing his amateur career with a perfect record of four wins and no losses.[[5]](#cite_note-TUF14fighters-5) He began his pro career in 2009, opening his career with a record of 4-0, all four won via stoppage. The wins earned him a fight for Russian promotion, [M-1 Global](/wiki/M-1_Global "M-1 Global"). The fight was held at the event M-1 Selection 2010 in New Jersey against Kevin Roddy. Bermudez controlled the entirety of the fight and won via unanimous decision. He was not selected to compete in the M-1 Global tournament, and never returned to fight for the promotion.

Bermudez returned to Pennsylvania Fighting Championships for one fight before signing with Shine Fights. Bermudez signed onto compete in Shine Fights 2010 Lightweight Grand Prix. His first fight in the tournament was against UFC veteran [Shannon Gugerty](/wiki/Shannon_Gugerty "Shannon Gugerty"). Bermudez won the fight via unanimous decision and moved onto fight UFC, MFC, Strikeforce, and DREAM veteran, [Drew Fickett](/wiki/Drew_Fickett "Drew Fickett"). Fickett handed Bermudez his first loss, submitting him in the first round via rear naked choke. He lost his second straight fight, also via rear naked choke, to undefeated [Jordan Rinaldi](/wiki/Jordan_Rinaldi "Jordan Rinaldi"). After the losses, Bermudez showed interest in dropping weight classes to compete at featherweight.

### The Ultimate Fighter

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Bermudez&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: The Ultimate Fighter")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b9/Dennis_Bermudez_at_UFC_Fight_Night_143.jpg/330px-Dennis_Bermudez_at_UFC_Fight_Night_143.jpg)](/wiki/File:Dennis_Bermudez_at_UFC_Fight_Night_143.jpg)

Dennis Bermudez's last fight at UFC Fight Night 143 post fight interview in New York, United States - Jan 19, 2019

In 2011, Bermudez signed with the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") to compete in [The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Bisping_vs._Team_Miller "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Bisping vs. Team Miller"). In the first episode, Bermudez fought reigning [King of the Cage](/wiki/King_of_the_Cage "King of the Cage") bantamweight champion, [Jimmie Rivera](/wiki/Jimmie_Rivera "Jimmie Rivera"), to gain entry into the *Ultimate Fighter* house. After being rocked in the first round, Bermudez defeated Rivera in the second round via TKO.

Bermudez was selected to be a part of [Team Miller](/wiki/Jason_Miller_(fighter) "Jason Miller (fighter)") as the team's first pick (second overall). In his quarterfinal bout, Bermudez fought Stephen Bass. Bermudez controlled the fight throughout round one before winning the fight in the second round via TKO.

Bermudez was selected to fight [Team Bisping](/wiki/Michael_Bisping "Michael Bisping") member [Akira Corassani](/wiki/Akira_Corassani "Akira Corassani") in the semi-finals. After being knocked down twice, Bermudez took Corassani down and finished the fight in the first round via submission due to a guillotine choke. With the win, Bermudez moved into the finals set to take place at [The Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_14_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale"). The submission also won Bermudez an additional $25,000 for the fan voted "Submission of the Season".

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Bermudez&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

Bermudez officially made his UFC debut on December 3, 2011, at [The Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_14_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter 14 Finale") against [Diego Brandão](/wiki/Diego_Brand%C3%A3o "Diego Brandão") of Team Bisping to determine the winner of [The Ultimate Fighter 14](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_14 "The Ultimate Fighter 14"). After a back-and-forth first round Bermudez knocked down Brandão and displayed strong ground and pound from the top position but while doing so Bermudez was caught in an armbar that ended the fight. The bout earned *Fight of the Night* honors, giving Bermudez an extra $40,000 in bonuses.[[7]](#cite_note-TUF14-7)

Bermudez next faced [Pablo Garza](/wiki/Pablo_Garza_(fighter) "Pablo Garza (fighter)") at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3") on May 5, 2012.[[8]](#cite_note-8) He won the fight via unanimous decision.

Bermudez fought Tommy Hayden on August 11, 2012, at [UFC 150](/wiki/UFC_150 "UFC 150").[[9]](#cite_note-9) Bermudez won late into round one after clipping Hayden with a front kick to the chest, a straight right to the face then catching him in a guillotine choke, causing Hayden to submit. The performance earned Bermudez *Submission of the Night* honors.[[10]](#cite_note-UFC150-10)

Bermudez next faced [Matt Grice](/wiki/Matt_Grice "Matt Grice") on February 23, 2013, at [UFC 157](/wiki/UFC_157 "UFC 157"). Bermudez won via split decision. The back and forth action earned both fighters *Fight of the Night* honors and much praise from MMA observers proclaiming it was an early front runner for "Fight of the Year".[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-UFC157-12)

Bermudez faced [Max Holloway](/wiki/Max_Holloway "Max Holloway") on May 25, 2013, at [UFC 160](/wiki/UFC_160 "UFC 160").[[13]](#cite_note-13) He won the fight via split decision. 11 out of 11 media outlets scored the bout to Holloway.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Bermudez was briefly scheduled to face [Nik Lentz](/wiki/Nik_Lentz "Nik Lentz") on November 6, 2013, at [UFC Fight Night 31](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_31 "UFC Fight Night 31"). However, Lentz was pulled from the pairing with Bermudez in favor of a bout with [Chad Mendes](/wiki/Chad_Mendes "Chad Mendes") on December 14, 2013, at [UFC on Fox 9](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_9 "UFC on Fox 9").[[15]](#cite_note-15) Bermudez instead fought [Steven Siler](/wiki/Steven_Siler "Steven Siler") as a replacement opponent.[[16]](#cite_note-16) He won the fight via unanimous decision.

Bermudez next faced [Jimy Hettes](/wiki/Jimy_Hettes "Jimy Hettes") on March 15, 2014, at [UFC 171](/wiki/UFC_171 "UFC 171"). He won the fight via TKO in the third round and won his first *Performance of the Night* bonus award.[[17]](#cite_note-UFC171-17)

In his highest profile fight to date, Bermudez faced [Clay Guida](/wiki/Clay_Guida "Clay Guida") on July 26, 2014, at [UFC on Fox 12](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_12 "UFC on Fox 12"). He won the bout via submission in the second round, and earned a *Performance of the Night* bonus award for the second time in a row.[[18]](#cite_note-UFConFox12-18)

Bermudez faced [Ricardo Lamas](/wiki/Ricardo_Lamas "Ricardo Lamas") on November 15, 2014, at [UFC 180](/wiki/UFC_180 "UFC 180"). He lost the fight via submission in the first round.[[19]](#cite_note-19)

Bermudez faced [Jeremy Stephens](/wiki/Jeremy_Stephens "Jeremy Stephens") on July 11, 2015, at [UFC 189](/wiki/UFC_189 "UFC 189"). After a close first two rounds, he lost the fight by TKO in the third round.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

Bermudez was expected to face [Maximo Blanco](/wiki/Maximo_Blanco "Maximo Blanco") on January 17, 2016, at [UFC Fight Night 81](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_81 "UFC Fight Night 81"). However, Bermudez pulled out of the fight in early December citing injury and was replaced by promotional newcomer [Luke Sanders](/wiki/Luke_Sanders "Luke Sanders").[[21]](#cite_note-21)[[22]](#cite_note-22) Following a quick recovery, Bermudez was rebooked and he faced [Tatsuya Kawajiri](/wiki/Tatsuya_Kawajiri "Tatsuya Kawajiri") on February 21, 2016, at [UFC Fight Night 83](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_83 "UFC Fight Night 83").[[23]](#cite_note-23) He won the fight by unanimous decision.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Bermudez faced [Rony Jason](/wiki/Rony_Jason "Rony Jason") on August 6, 2016, at [UFC Fight Night 92](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_92 "UFC Fight Night 92"). He won the fight by unanimous decision.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

Bermudez faced returning veteran [Chan Sung Jung](/wiki/Chan_Sung_Jung "Chan Sung Jung") on February 4, 2017, at [UFC Fight Night 104](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_104 "UFC Fight Night 104"). He lost the fight by knockout in the first round.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

Bermudez faced [Darren Elkins](/wiki/Darren_Elkins "Darren Elkins") on July 22, 2017, at [UFC on Fox 25](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_25 "UFC on Fox 25").[[27]](#cite_note-27) He lost the back-and-forth fight via split decision.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

Bermudez faced [Andre Fili](/wiki/Andre_Fili "Andre Fili") on January 27, 2018 at [UFC on Fox 27](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_27 "UFC on Fox 27").[[29]](#cite_note-29) He lost the fight via split decision.[[30]](#cite_note-30)

Bermudez faced [Rick Glenn](/wiki/Rick_Glenn "Rick Glenn") on July 14, 2018 at [UFC Fight Night 133](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_133 "UFC Fight Night 133").[[31]](#cite_note-31) He lost the fight via split decision.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

Bermudez faced Te Edwards in a lightweight bout on January 19, 2019 at [UFC Fight Night 143](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_143 "UFC Fight Night 143").[[33]](#cite_note-33)[[34]](#cite_note-34) He won the fight via unanimous decision. He announced his retirement after the fight, leaving his gloves in the Octagon.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Bermudez&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Bermudez is of [Puerto Rican](/wiki/Nuyorican "Nuyorican") and [British](/wiki/British_Americans "British Americans") descent.[[36]](#cite_note-36) He has three children: two sons named Bryson and Maddox (with his then-girlfriend Lindsey Kuhn),[[37]](#cite_note-37) and a daughter named Lulu (with his current partner Nina Sea).[[38]](#cite_note-38)

Bermudez graduated from [Bloomsburg University of Pennsylvania](/wiki/Bloomsburg_University_of_Pennsylvania "Bloomsburg University of Pennsylvania") in 2009 with a degree in [anthropology](/wiki/Anthropology "Anthropology").[[39]](#cite_note-39)

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Dennis_Bermudez&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + Submission of the Season ([The Ultimate Fighter 14](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_14 "The Ultimate Fighter 14")) vs. [Akira Corassani](/wiki/Akira_Corassani "Akira Corassani")
  + [Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") (Two times) vs. [Diego Brandão](/wiki/Diego_Brand%C3%A3o "Diego Brandão") and [Matt Grice](/wiki/Matt_Grice "Matt Grice")[[7]](#cite_note-TUF14-7)[[12]](#cite_note-UFC157-12)
  + [Submission of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") (One time) vs. Tommy Hayden[[10]](#cite_note-UFC150-10)
  + [Performance of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") (Two times) vs. [Jimy Hettes](/wiki/Jimy_Hettes "Jimy Hettes") and [Clay Guida](/wiki/Clay_Guida "Clay Guida")[[17]](#cite_note-UFC171-17)[[18]](#cite_note-UFConFox12-18)
  + Tied ([Brian Ortega](/wiki/Brian_Ortega "Brian Ortega")) for fifth most submission attempts in UFC Featherweight division history (12)[[40]](#cite_note-ufcfw-40)
  + Second most control time in UFC Featherweight division history (1:10:49)[[40]](#cite_note-ufcfw-40)
  + Third most takedowns landed in UFC Featherweight division history (46)[[40]](#cite_note-ufcfw-40)
  + Fourth most top position time in UFC Featherweight division history (45:29)[[40]](#cite_note-ufcfw-40)
  + Tied ([Jesse Ronson](/wiki/Jesse_Ronson "Jesse Ronson") & [Zhalgas Zhumagulov](/wiki/Zhalgas_Zhumagulov "Zhalgas Zhumagulov")) for most consecutive split decision losses in UFC history (3)
  + **UFC.com Awards**
    - 2013: [Ranked #6 Fight of the Year](/wiki/2013_in_UFC#2013_UFC.com_awards "2013 in UFC") vs. [Matt Grice](/wiki/Matt_Grice "Matt Grice")[[41]](#cite_note-41)
